old_claim.note: Telephone Exchange provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Standard DSL broadband scaled by reusing carrier telephone access infrastructure: metallic twisted-pair subscriber loops and central-office/network-operator equipment.

source_shape.source_locator: Historical significance section: ETHW says Alcatel ADSL deployed over legacy copper telephone lines and took full benefit of existing copper lines and Central Offices.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: DSL broadband scaled by using existing telephone-carrier copper access plant and central-office side equipment.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports a commercial/scaling dependency rather than a hard requirement because the graph lacks a separate local-loop/central-office node and telephone_exchange is the nearest infrastructure proxy.

ontology_before: The graph treated telephone exchange infrastructure as a generic enabling prerequisite.
ontology_after: The graph treats telephone access infrastructure as the commercial scaling substrate for DSL deployment.
would_reject_if: A more specific copper_local_loop or DSLAM node is added and replaces telephone_exchange as the direct infrastructure dependency.
The node were rescoped to lab-only ADSL prototypes rather than standardized access-network deployment.
short_reason: Telephone access plant is scaling infrastructure for DSL.
